analysis.consistency: High. The spell follows standard D&D 5e spell structure (level, school, casting time, range, components, duration, description, classes). The mechanical progression (restrained -> failed saves -> petrified) is clearly laid out.
analysis.quality: High. The description is detailed and handles edge cases (tracking successes/failures, effect of breaking while petrified, permanent effect if concentration is held). The flavor aligns well with the mechanics.

mechanics.progression: If restrained, subsequent Constitution saves determine progress toward petrification (3 successes end it, 3 failures cause it).
mechanics.tracking_mechanism: The 'successes and failures don't need to be consecutive' is a crucial and well-documented mechanic for this specific spell, preventing easy exploits.
mechanics.final_effect: Petrified condition, with a specific note on physical damage consequence upon reversal.
mechanics.duration_interaction: If concentration is maintained for the full 1 minute (10 turns), the petrification becomes permanent until removed (implying the save progression is bypassed or fixed), which is a significant power spike matching the sustained concentration cost.

analysis.notes: The spell successfully blends a duration-based condition (Restrained) that can lead to a potent, long-term condition (Petrified). The rule about maintaining concentration for the full duration leading to permanent effect is key to its balance.
